info.title: Weave
info.description: W&B Weave API for LLM observability, tracing, evaluations, and datasets

get.description:
Lookup the geographic location of a user based on their IP address.

This API exists for debugging purposes and may not be available in the future.

parameters.name: ip

schema.description: IP address to geolocate, defaults to client IP address
schema.title: Ip

parameters.description: IP address to geolocate, defaults to client IP address

get.description:
Returns a list of available models for Artificial Analysis.

This API is available without authentication.

Returns a list of models that are available to be used with OpenRouter.

This API is available without authentication.

Returns available hardware and pricing for a given model.

Called by NVIDIA to show users their options and redirect them
based on what we support.  Only serverless options are returned.

parameters.name: model

schema.description: Model name without the publisher prefix
schema.title: Model

parameters.description: Model name without the publisher prefix

post.description: Compute per-call usage for a trace, with descendant rollup.
Compute aggregated usage for multiple root calls, with descendant rollup.

put.description: Add tags to an object version.

post.description: Remove tags from an object version.

put.description: Set aliases for an object version.

get.description: List all tags in a project.
List all aliases in a project.

post.description: Add feedback to a call or object.
Add multiple feedback items to calls or objects.
Return aggregated feedback statistics over time buckets.
Discover feedback payload schema (paths and types) from sample rows.

schema.title: Response Projects Info Service Projects Info Post

post.description: Query annotation queues for a project (streaming NDJSON response).

put.description: Update an annotation queue's metadata (name, description, scorer_refs).

post.description: Add calls to an annotation queue.
Query items in an annotation queue with pagination and sorting.
Get stats for multiple annotation queues.
Update the annotation state of a queue item for the current annotator.

Request body for updating annotation progress (queue_id and item_id come from path).

AnnotationQueueItemsFilter.description:
Simple filter for annotation queue items.

Supports equality filtering on call metadata fields and IN filtering on annotation state.

Request body for updating an annotation queue (queue_id comes from path).

sort_by.description: Sort by multiple fields (e.g., created_at, updated_at, name)

AnnotationQueuesQueryReq.description: Request to query annotation queues for a project.

queue_ids.title: Queue Ids

AnnotationQueuesStatsReq.description: Request to get stats for multiple annotation queues.

AnnotationQueuesStatsRes.description: Response with stats for multiple annotation queues.

percentiles.title: Percentiles
percentiles.description: Percentile values to compute (0-100). E.g., [50, 95, 99] for p50, p95, p99

CallMetricSpec.title: CallMetricSpec
CallMetricSpec.description: Specification for a call-level metric to aggregate (not grouped by model).

end.title: End
end.description: Exclusive end time (UTC, ISO 8601). Defaults to now if omitted.

granularity.title: Granularity
granularity.description: Bucket size in seconds (e.g., 3600 for 1 hour). If omitted, auto-selected based on time range. Will be adjusted if it would produce more than 10,000 buckets.

usage_metrics.title: Usage Metrics
usage_metrics.description: Usage metrics (tokens, cost) to compute. Grouped by timestamp and model.

call_metrics.title: Call Metrics
call_metrics.description: Call-level metrics (latency, counts) to compute. Grouped by timestamp only.

CallStatsReq.title: CallStatsReq
CallStatsReq.description: Request for aggregated call statistics over a time range.

usage_buckets.title: Usage Buckets
usage_buckets.description: Usage metrics by model. Each bucket contains 'timestamp', 'model', and aggregated metric values.

call_buckets.title: Call Buckets
call_buckets.description: Call-level metrics. Each bucket contains 'timestamp' and aggregated metric values.

wb_user_id.title: Wb User Id
wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

wb_user_id.title: Wb User Id
wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

include_costs.title: Include Costs
include_costs.description: Beta, subject to change. If true, the response will include any model costs for each call.

include_feedback.title: Include Feedback
include_feedback.description: Beta, subject to change. If true, the response will include feedback for each call.

include_storage_size.title: Include Storage Size
include_storage_size.description: Beta, subject to change. If true, the response will include the storage size for a call.

include_total_storage_size.title: Include Total Storage Size
include_total_storage_size.description: Beta, subject to change. If true, the response will include the total storage size for a trace.

include_usernames.title: Include Usernames
include_usernames.description: If true, the response will attempt to resolve each call's wb_user_id to a username for the duration of this request.

expand_columns.title: Expand Columns
expand_columns.description: Columns to expand, i.e. refs to other objects

return_expanded_column_values.title: Return Expanded Column Values
return_expanded_column_values.description: If true, the response will include raw values for expanded columns. If false, the response expand_columns will only be used for filtering and ordering. This is useful for clients that want to resolve refs themselves, e.g. for performance reasons.

expand_columns.title: Expand Columns
expand_columns.description: Columns with refs to objects or table rows that require expansion during filtering or ordering.

call_ids.title: Call Ids
call_ids.description: List of call IDs to score

scorer_refs.title: Scorer Refs
scorer_refs.description: List of scorer refs to apply

wb_user_id.title: Wb User Id
wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

CallsScoreReq.title: CallsScoreReq
CallsScoreReq.description:
Request to enqueue scoring jobs for a list of calls.

Scoring is performed asynchronously by the call_scoring_worker, which
consumes messages from Kafka and applies each scorer_ref to each call_id.

CallsScoreRes.title: CallsScoreRes
CallsScoreRes.description:
Empty response for calls_score.

Defined as a model (rather than returning None) to follow the convention
used throughout this interface and to allow fields to be added later
without a breaking change.

call_ids.title: Call Ids
call_ids.description: Root call IDs to aggregate. Each result key corresponds to one input call ID.

include_costs.title: Include Costs
include_costs.description: If true, include cost calculations in the usage.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of calls to process across all traces. Acts as a safety limit to prevent unbounded memory usage.

CallsUsageReq.title: CallsUsageReq
CallsUsageReq.description:
Request to compute aggregated usage for multiple root calls.

This endpoint returns usage metrics for each requested root call, where each
root's metrics include the sum of its own usage plus all descendants' usage.

Note: All matching calls are loaded into memory for aggregation. For very large
result sets (>10k calls), consider batching root call IDs or using narrower
filters at the application layer.

CallsUsageRes.title: CallsUsageRes
CallsUsageRes.description: Response with aggregated usage metrics per root call.

ContainsOperation.title: ContainsOperation

ContainsSpec.title: ContainsSpec
ContainsSpec.description:
Specification for the `$contains` operation.

- `input`: The string to search.
- `substr`: The substring to search for.
- `case_insensitive`: If true, match is case-insensitive.

ConvertOperation.title: ConvertOperation

ConvertSpec.title: ConvertSpec
ConvertSpec.description:
Specifies conversion details for `$convert`.

- `input`: The operand to convert.
- `to`: The type to convert to.

completion_token_cost_unit.description: The unit of the cost for the completion tokens

effective_date.title: Effective Date
effective_date.description: The date after which the cost is effective for, will default to the current date if not provided

provider_id.title: Provider Id
provider_id.description: The provider of the LLM, e.g. 'openai' or 'mistral'. If not provided, the provider_id will be set to 'default'

wb_user_id.title: Wb User Id
wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

EqOperation.title: EqOperation
EqOperation.description:
Equality check between two operands.

Example:
    ```
    {
        "$eq": [{"$getField": "op_name"}, {"$literal": "predict"}]
    }
    ```

evaluation_call_ids.title: Evaluation Call Ids
evaluation_call_ids.description: Evaluation root call IDs to include.

evaluation_run_ids.title: Evaluation Run Ids
evaluation_run_ids.description: Alias for evaluation call IDs from the Evaluation Runs API.

require_intersection.title: Require Intersection
require_intersection.description: When true, only include rows present in all requested evaluations.

include_raw_data_rows.title: Include Raw Data Rows
include_raw_data_rows.description: When true, populate raw_data_row on each result row. Inline rows are returned as their dict value; dataset-referenced rows are returned as the ref string unless resolve_row_refs is also true.

resolve_row_refs.title: Resolve Row Refs
resolve_row_refs.description: When true (requires include_raw_data_rows=True), resolve dataset-row reference strings to actual row data via a table lookup. When false, dataset-row refs are returned as-is.

include_rows.title: Include Rows
include_rows.description: When true, include grouped row/trial data in `rows` and compute `total_rows` for the requested row-level view.

include_summary.title: Include Summary
include_summary.description: When true, include aggregated scorer/evaluation summary data in `summary`.

summary_require_intersection.title: Summary Require Intersection
summary_require_intersection.description: Optional intersection behavior for the summary section. When null, the value of `require_intersection` is used.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Optional row-level page size applied after grouping and intersection.

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: Optional row-level page offset applied after grouping and intersection.

warnings.title: Warnings
warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings (e.g. failed to resolve dataset row refs).

path.title: Path
path.description: Dot-joined subpath for nested dimensions, e.g. 'passed' for token_distance.passed. None for root-level scalar scorers.

value_type.title: Value Type
value_type.description: Type of the leaf value: binary (bool) or continuous (number).

EvalResultsScorerStats.description: Stats for a single flattened score dimension (scorer_key or scorer_key.path.to.leaf).

wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

id.title: Id
id.description: If provided by the client, this ID will be used for the feedback row instead of a server-generated one.

queue_id.title: Queue Id
queue_id.description: The annotation queue ID this feedback was created from. References annotation_queues.id. NULL when feedback is created outside of queues.

wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

value_type.title: Value Type
value_type.description: Type of value at path. numeric: avg/min/max; boolean: count_true/count_false.

aggregations.title: Aggregations
aggregations.description: Aggregation functions to compute. If empty, defaults are chosen based on value_type: numeric->avg/min/max, boolean->count_true/count_false.

percentiles.title: Percentiles
percentiles.description: Percentile values to compute (0–100), e.g. [5, 50, 95]. Only applicable for numeric value_type fields; ignored for boolean/categorical.

FeedbackMetricSpec.description: Specification for a feedback payload metric to aggregate.

value_type.title: Value Type
value_type.description: Inferred type of value at path.

FeedbackPayloadPath.title: FeedbackPayloadPath
FeedbackPayloadPath.description: Discovered path in feedback payload with inferred type.

end.title: End
end.description: Exclusive end time (UTC, ISO 8601). Defaults to now if omitted.

trigger_ref.title: Trigger Ref
trigger_ref.description: Filter by trigger_ref (exact or prefix match for all-versions).

sample_limit.title: Sample Limit
sample_limit.description: Max distinct trigger_refs to sample when discovering the payload schema. Each distinct trigger_ref (monitor/source) typically has a fixed payload structure, so sampling one payload per ref is usually enough to see the full schema. 2 000 covers virtually all real-world projects while keeping the query fast; the hard cap of 5 000 prevents runaway scans.

FeedbackPayloadSchemaReq.description: Request for feedback payload schema discovery.

paths.title: Paths
paths.description: Discovered leaf paths with inferred value types.

FeedbackPayloadSchemaRes.description: Response with discovered feedback payload paths and types.

id.description: If provided by the client, this ID will be used for the feedback row instead of a server-generated one.

wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

end.title: End
end.description: Exclusive end time (UTC, ISO 8601). Defaults to now if omitted.

trigger_ref.title: Trigger Ref
trigger_ref.description: Filter by trigger_ref (exact or prefix match for all-versions).

granularity.title: Granularity
granularity.description: Bucket size in seconds. If omitted, auto-selected based on time range.

FeedbackStatsReq.description: Request for aggregated feedback statistics over time buckets.

start.description: Resolved start time (always UTC, regardless of the requested timezone).

end.title: End
end.description: Resolved end time (always UTC, regardless of the requested timezone).

buckets.title: Buckets
buckets.description: Time-bucketed aggregations. Each dict has 'timestamp' (ISO string), 'count' (int), and '{agg}_{slug}' keys for each requested metric+aggregation.

window_stats.title: Window Stats
window_stats.description: Aggregations over the full query window, keyed by metric slug (e.g. 'output_score'). Each value maps agg name to result.

country_name.title: Country Name

ip.title: Ip
ip.description: Resolved IP address, useful for debugging

location.description: Information about the location of the IP address, None if could not be determined

allowed.title: Allowed
allowed.description: Whether the IP address is allowed to be used for inference.

GetFieldOperator.title: GetFieldOperator
GetFieldOperator.description:
Access a field on the traced call.

Supports dot notation for nested access, e.g. `summary.usage.tokens`.

Only works on fields present in the `CallSchema`, including:
- Top-level fields like `op_name`, `trace_id`, `started_at`
- Nested fields like `inputs.input_name`, `summary.usage.tokens`, etc.

Example:
    ```
    {"$getField": "op_name"}
    ```

GteOperation.title: GteOperation
GteOperation.description:
Greater than or equal comparison.

Example:
    ```
    {
        "$gte": [{"$getField": "summary.usage.tokens"}, {"$literal": 100}]
    }
    ```

wb_user_id.title: Wb User Id
wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

track_llm_call.title: Track Llm Call
track_llm_call.description: Whether to track this image generation call in the trace server

InOperation.title: InOperation
InOperation.description:
Membership check.

Returns true if the left operand is in the list provided as the second operand.

Example:
    ```
    {
        "$in": [
            {"$getField": "op_name"},
            [{"$literal": "predict"}, {"$literal": "generate"}]
        ]
    }
    ```

LLMAggregatedUsage.title: LLMAggregatedUsage
LLMAggregatedUsage.description: Aggregated usage metrics for a specific LLM.

LiteralOperation.title: LiteralOperation
LiteralOperation.description:
Represents a constant value in the query language.

This can be any standard JSON-serializable value.

Example:
    ```
    {"$literal": "predict"}
    ```

LteOperation.title: LteOperation
LteOperation.description:
Less than or equal comparison.

Example:
    ```
    {
        "$lte": [{"$getField": "summary.usage.tokens"}, {"$literal": 100}]
    }
    ```

metadata_only.title: Metadata Only

wb_user_id.title: Wb User Id
wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

expected_digest.title: Expected Digest
expected_digest.description: Client-computed digest for server-side validation. If provided, the server will verify it matches the server-computed digest.

wb_user_id.title: Wb User Id
wb_user_id.description: Do not set directly. Server will automatically populate this field.

ObjTagsBody.description: Request body for adding/removing tags (object_id and digest come from path).

base_object_classes.title: Base Object Classes
base_object_classes.description: Filter objects by their base classes

exclude_base_object_classes.title: Exclude Base Object Classes
exclude_base_object_classes.description: Exclude objects by their base classes

leaf_object_classes.title: Leaf Object Classes
leaf_object_classes.description: Filter objects by their leaf classes

is_op.title: Is Op
is_op.description: Filter objects based on whether they are weave.ops or not. `True` will only return ops, `False` will return non-ops, and `None` will return all objects

latest_only.title: Latest Only
latest_only.description: If True, return only the latest version of each object. `False` and `None` will return all versions

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Filter object versions that have any of the specified tags

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: Filter objects that have any of the specified aliases

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of this op. Ops with the same name will be versioned together.

source_code.title: Source Code
source_code.description: Complete source code for this op, including imports

OpCreateBody.title: OpCreateBody
OpCreateBody.description:
Request body for creating an Op object via REST API.

This model excludes project_id since it comes from the URL path in RESTful endpoints.

digest.title: Digest
digest.description: The digest of the created op

object_id.title: Object Id
object_id.description: The ID of the created op

version_index.title: Version Index
version_index.description: The version index of the created op

OpCreateRes.description: Response model for creating an Op object.

num_deleted.title: Num Deleted
num_deleted.description: Number of op versions deleted from this op

version_index.title: Version Index
version_index.description: The version index of this op

OpReadRes.description:
Response model for reading an Op object.

The code field contains the actual source code of the op.

Pricing.title: Pricing
Pricing.description:
All pricing values are in USD per 1 token.

Pricing fields are in string format to avoid floating point precision issues.

deprecation_date.title: Deprecation Date
deprecation_date.description: Date when the model is deprecated (YYYY-MM-DD). Omitted from output if not set.

evaluation_run_id.description: Optional evaluation run ID to link this score as a child call

row_digests.description: The digests of the rows that were created

digest.title: Digest
digest.description: The digest of the table to query

filter.description: Optional filter to apply to the query. See `TableRowFilter` for more details.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: Number of rows to skip before starting to return rows

sort_by.title: Sort By
sort_by.description: List of fields to sort by. Fields can be dot-separated to access dictionary values. No sorting uses the default table order (insertion order).

digests.title: Digests
digests.description: The digests of the tables to query

include_storage_size.title: Include Storage Size
include_storage_size.description: If true, the `storage_size_bytes` column is returned.

digest.title: Digest
digest.description: The digest of the table to query

row_digests.title: Row Digests

expected_digest.description: Client-computed table digest for server-side validation.

updated_row_digests.title: Updated Row Digests
updated_row_digests.description: The digests of the rows that were updated

after_datetime.title: After Datetime
after_datetime.description: Only include threads with start_time after this timestamp

before_datetime.title: Before Datetime
before_datetime.description: Only include threads with last_updated before this timestamp

thread_ids.title: Thread Ids
thread_ids.description: Only include threads with thread_ids in this list

filter.description: Filter criteria for the threads query

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of threads to return

sort_by.title: Sort By
sort_by.description: Sorting criteria for the threads. Supported fields: 'thread_id', 'turn_count', 'start_time', 'last_updated', 'p50_turn_duration_ms', 'p99_turn_duration_ms'.

ThreadsQueryReq.title: ThreadsQueryReq
ThreadsQueryReq.description:
Query threads with aggregated statistics based on turn calls only.

Turn calls are the immediate children of thread contexts (where call.id == turn_id).
This provides meaningful conversation-level statistics rather than including all
nested implementation details.

filter.description: Filter to select calls. Typically use trace_ids to get all calls in a trace.

query.description: Additional query conditions for filtering calls.

include_costs.title: Include Costs
include_costs.description: If true, include cost calculations in the usage.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of calls to process. Acts as a safety limit to prevent unbounded memory usage.

TraceUsageReq.title: TraceUsageReq
TraceUsageReq.description:
Request to compute per-call usage for a trace, with descendant rollup.

This endpoint returns usage metrics for each call in the trace, where each
call's metrics include the sum of its own usage plus all descendants' usage.
Use this for trace view where you want to see rolled-up metrics per call.

Note: All matching calls are loaded into memory for aggregation. For very large
result sets (>10k calls), consider using more specific filters or pagination
at the application layer.

TraceUsageRes.title: TraceUsageRes
TraceUsageRes.description: Response with per-call usage metrics (each includes descendant contributions).

metric.title: Metric
metric.description: Metric to aggregate. Token metrics are normalized across providers.

percentiles.title: Percentiles
percentiles.description: Percentile values to compute (0-100). E.g., [50, 95, 99] for p50, p95, p99

UsageMetricSpec.title: UsageMetricSpec
UsageMetricSpec.description: Specification for a usage metric to aggregate (grouped by model).
